one_liner: CMG at $34.60 approaching Wyckoff resistance ($35.78) with RSI 62.2 and MACD positive — but DCF shows 57% overvaluation at $14.74 fair value; earnings July 29 with near-term consumer caution.

price_action.intraday_story: CMG opened at $33.43 and ran all day to $34.62 — essentially at Wyckoff resistance of $35.78 at the close. RSI 62.2 and approaching resistance makes this a natural pause point.

news_catalyst.headline: Chipotle Launches PGA Tour 2K25 Rewards — Consumer Engagement Initiative
news_catalyst.summary: CMG launched a gaming/rewards partnership — a minor consumer engagement item, not a fundamental catalyst. The +3.5% gain appears to be sector rotation or technical momentum. DCF remains deeply negative vs current price. Earnings July 29.

technical.summary: CMG pressing Wyckoff resistance at $35.78 with RSI 62.2. DCF at $14.74 (57% overvalued) makes this an avoid for fundamental investors. Earnings July 29.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Approaching Wyckoff resistance at $35.78. RSI 62.2 — room to press but DCF gap too large to justify adding.

valuation.summary: DCF $14.74 vs $34.60 — 57% overvalued. Chipotle premium reflects brand and unit growth optionality but the current price requires exceptional execution for years.

key_strengths: Unit growth pipeline strong — 2,000+ locations planned
key_concerns: Consumer spending caution in QSR category
fundamentals.summary: C grade. Premium brand but significantly overvalued at current levels. Avoid.

risks: Consumer spending slowdown in QSR would hit same-store sales growth
